commit.message:
chore: disable AKS auto-scaling for workshop environment

Workshop infrastructure does not need HA. AKS node pools already use
1 node each with zone_redundant=false on Cosmos DB and LRS storage.
The only remaining HA setting was AKS auto-scaling (max 3/5 nodes).

Disable auto-scaling in dev.tfvars and both init scripts so new
environments default to fixed 1-node pools.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add ConfigurationSchema.json for Azure.Identity.Broker (#57526)

Add schema/ConfigurationSchema.json to Azure.Identity.Broker defining
the credential sources that require the Broker package:

- BrokerCredential
- VisualStudioCodeCredential

These were removed from Azure.Identity's schema in #57483 since they
require installation of the Azure.Identity.Broker package to function.
The Broker package's own schema ensures IntelliSense only surfaces
these credentials when the Broker package is referenced.

Closes #57521

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: align infra, docs, and diagrams with architectural intent

Embedding model: standardize on text-embedding-3-small across all docs,
Terraform variables, init scripts, and architecture diagram (was
inconsistently referencing text-embedding-ada-002 in several places).

Chat model: update Program.cs default from gpt-4o-mini to gpt-4.1 to
match the deployed model.

Architecture diagram: remove Future Agent placeholder node and its
connecting edge from architecture.drawio.

Network policies: fix communication graph to match README traffic paths.
Both CRM Agent and Product Agent use both MCP servers (CRM MCP +
Knowledge MCP), so ingress/egress rules are updated for all six
service policies:
- crm-mcp: allow ingress from product-agent (was crm-agent only)
- knowledge-mcp: allow ingress from crm-agent (was product-agent only)
- crm-agent: add knowledge-mcp egress
- product-agent: add crm-mcp egress
- bff-api: add crm-api egress for direct data proxy path
- crm-api: allow ingress from bff-api (was crm-mcp only)

Documentation: update security.md network policy table,
network-policies/README.md traffic diagram and policy table to match.

Business scenario: add missing Agent route annotation to Scenario 6
(Mike Johnson — gear care) routing to CRM Agent.

Cosmos DB: add consent-records container to agents account (referenced
in security.md consent model but was missing from Terraform).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add ConfigurationSchema.json for Azure.Identity credential IntelliSense (#57483)

* Add ConfigurationSchema.json for Azure.Identity credential IntelliSense

Add JSON schema to provide IntelliSense for credential configuration in
appsettings.json files. Defines credential sources (AzureCliCredential,
ManagedIdentityCredential, WorkloadIdentityCredential, etc.) with
conditional properties per credential type using draft-07 if/then patterns.

Includes secret-blocking patterns for ClientSecret, ClientCertificatePassword,
and Password to prevent secrets from being placed in configuration files.

Closes #56781

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add TokenFilePath to WorkloadIdentityCredential schema

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ManagedIdentityAsFederatedIdentityCredential schema

Remove SystemAssigned from ManagedIdentityIdKind enum (runtime rejects it).
Make ManagedIdentityIdKind and ManagedIdentityId required since both are
always needed for this credential type.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add secret-blocking patterns to chainableCredential

Add ClientSecret, ClientCertificatePassword, and Password blocking to
chainableCredential definition for parity with the top-level credential.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Mark AzurePipelinesCredential required fields

TenantId, ClientId, AzurePipelinesServiceConnectionId, and
AzurePipelinesSystemAccessToken are all required at runtime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add explicit type string to CredentialSource properties

* Require Sources for ChainedTokenCredential

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove required from if/then blocks (not enforced by IDEs)

Required constraints inside if/then conditionals are not enforced by VS
IntelliSense. Removing until root cause is investigated. See #57508.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Alphabetize CredentialSource enum lists

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add TimeSpan example to CredentialProcessTimeout description

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clarify federated identity description as user-assigned

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix AzurePowerShellCredential timeout default to 10 seconds

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clarify ManagedIdentityId as user-assigned in ManagedIdentityCredential

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename CredentialProcessTimeout to ProcessTimeout in schema and binder

Use ProcessTimeout as the config key to match the concrete credential
options property name rather than the DAC-specific name. DAC's public
CredentialProcessTimeout property is unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add missing EnvironmentCredential config properties

Add ClientId, ClientCertificatePath, SendCertificateChain, and Username
to EnvironmentCredential schema. These are non-secret properties already
handled by the configuration binder.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add BrowserCustomization to InteractiveBrowserCredential schema

Add browserCustomizationOptions definition with SuccessMessage and
ErrorMessage properties, referenced from InteractiveBrowserCredential
in both credential and chainableCredential sections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ProcessTimeout defaults per credential type

AzureCliCredential: 13s, AzureDeveloperCliCredential: 13s,
AzurePowerShellCredential: 10s, VisualStudioCredential: 30s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove Username config binding for EnvironmentCredential

Username supports the deprecated ROPC flow (UsernamePasswordCredential)
which is incompatible with MFA. Remove from schema, config binder,
factories, and tests. EnvironmentCredential still reads AZURE_USERNAME
from env vars via EnvironmentCredentialOptions defaults.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove BrokerCredential and VisualStudioCodeCredential from schema

Both require Azure.Identity.Broker package to function. Their schema
fragments belong in Azure.Identity.Broker's ConfigurationSchema.json.
See #57521.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename AzurePipelines config keys to match credential properties

Rename AzurePipelinesServiceConnectionId -> ServiceConnectionId and
AzurePipelinesSystemAccessToken -> SystemAccessToken to match the
AzurePipelinesCredential property names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove Password config binding and update docs

Remove Password binding from EnvironmentCredential (deprecated ROPC
flow). Remove Password secret-blocking pattern from schema. Update
configuration docs to reflect all property renames and credential
changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert EnvironmentUsername/Password from DAC factory and options

These were added in #56721 as part of config propagation, not
pre-existing. EnvironmentCredential reads AZURE_USERNAME/AZURE_PASSWORD
directly from env vars via EnvironmentCredentialOptions defaults.

- Add OrderService, TicketService, QrCodeService
- Add 17 controllers (public, auth, buyer, organizer, admin,
  checkout, payment) using thin controller / service pattern
- Add 45 routes with auth + role-based access groups
- Add 26 Blade views with Tailwind CSS CDN (Spanish UI)
- Add config/tickets.php for configurable 5% service fee
- Update DatabaseSeeder with categories, venues, users,
  organizer profile, and 3 published events with ticket types
- Add MP_PUBLIC_KEY/MP_ACCESS_TOKEN/MP_WEBHOOK_SECRET to
  .env.example and config/services.php (Mercado Pago)

base model properties (name, location, tags from TrackedResource/Resource)

Feature/debate Turn By Turn (#18)

* feat: turn-by-turn debate with typewriter animation

Replace batch /chat/debate with sequential /debate/turn calls so the
debate feels live: typing indicator appears while waiting for each API
response, then the reply typewriters in character-by-character before
the next agent begins. Frontend maintains the history array and passes
it to each call so each twin can respond to the prior exchange.

Backend: add DebateHistoryEntry + DebateTurnRequest models and
POST /debate/turn endpoint; each call runs a single DebateAgent turn
via asyncio.to_thread.

Terraform: add POST /debate/turn API Gateway route.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use Field(default_factory=list) for Pydantic mutable default

Avoids shared mutable default list on DebateTurnRequest.history.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: catch broad Exception in debate/turn endpoint

Add a generic Exception handler alongside the existing ClientError handler
so unexpected runtime errors (KeyError, unexpected response shape, etc.)
return a controlled HTTPException 500 instead of bubbling up as unhandled errors.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: clear animateText interval on component unmount

Track setInterval id in intervalRef and clear it in a useEffect
cleanup so navigating away mid-animation doesn't leave a dangling
interval updating state on an unmounted component.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update frontend/app/debate/page.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: filter empty segments in initials to handle extra spaces

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add bounds validation on debate history entries

Cap history to 12 entries max and enforce character limits on
twin_name (100) and text (2000) per entry to prevent oversized
prompts and potential abuse.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add DebateTurnResponse model to /debate/turn endpoint

Defines an explicit DebateTurnResponse Pydantic model and wires it
as response_model on the POST /debate/turn decorator. Improves OpenAPI
docs and ensures FastAPI validates/serializes the response shape.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update frontend/app/debate/page.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: clear intervalRef in reset() to prevent stray animations

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: suppress error on intentional fetch cancellation in startDebate

- Add AbortController tied to reset() and unmount to cancel in-flight fetches
- Pass controller.signal to the /debate/turn fetch call
- Check cancelledRef.current in the catch block before setting debateError
  so user-initiated cancellations don't surface as errors

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: derive _MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES from DEBATE_ROUNDS env var

_MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES was hardcoded to 12 but DEBATE_ROUNDS can be
configured via env var on both backend and frontend. Now both read
from DEBATE_ROUNDS / NEXT_PUBLIC_DEBATE_ROUNDS respectively, and
_MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES is derived as DEBATE_ROUNDS * 2 + 4 so the
history cap scales automatically with the configured round count.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guard startDebate() against double-trigger with pageState check

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update backend/server.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: parse DEBATE_ROUNDS env var defensively with clamping

Wrap the int() conversion in a try/except so a bad env var value
(empty string, non-numeric) falls back to the default (2) instead
of crashing the app at startup. Also clamp to [1, 4] to prevent
accidentally configuring extremely large debates via env var.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: throttle auto-scroll to fire on turn start/end only, not every typewriter tick

* fix: guard DEBATE_MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES parse with try/except

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: JSON-escape client-provided fields in debate turn prompt

Prevent quotes and newlines in twin_name/text/topic from breaking the
prompt structure by escaping via json.dumps before interpolation.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: align DEBATE_ROUNDS default to 3 and clarify comment

- Default was 2 (backend) vs 3 (frontend NEXT_PUBLIC_DEBATE_ROUNDS),
  which was inconsistent and the comment was misleading.
- Updated comment to clarify DEBATE_ROUNDS only controls the batched
  /chat/debate endpoint, not the turn-by-turn /debate/turn endpoint.
- Aligned backend default to 3 to match the frontend default.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: revert DEBATE_ROUNDS default to 2 to stay within API Gateway timeout

Default of 3 rounds = 6 sequential Bedrock calls at ~4-5s each ≈ 24-30s,
which leaves only a slim margin before the API Gateway 30s hard timeout.
Reverting to 2 (4 calls ≈ 20-28s) gives a comfortable safety buffer.
Operators who need 3 rounds can set DEBATE_ROUNDS=3 via env var.

Co-authored-by: Siddharth <sagacioussid02@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: lower history defaults and add total character budget truncation

- Lower _MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES default from 40 to 20 (still generous for >10 rounds)
- Lower _MAX_HISTORY_TEXT_LEN from 2000 to 1000 (3-5 sentences is ~300-500 chars)
- Add _MAX_HISTORY_TOTAL_CHARS=8000 budget enforced server-side before prompt build
- Server-side truncation drops oldest history entries (not newest) to stay within budget
- Use truncated history list consistently for both history_lines and last entry

- patch_apply.sh: Apply staged patches to a review branch

- patch_test.sh: Run build and unit tests, collect silicon results
- patch_integrate.sh: Cherry-pick reviewed patches to working branch

All bash scripts:
- Provide zero-dependency alternatives to Python versions
- Generate compatible JSON output for downstream steps
- Support same configuration via .patch-pipeline.toml and env vars
- Can be mixed with Python scripts in the same pipeline
- Include comprehensive error handling and user-friendly output

Added BASH_README.md with detailed usage guide and examples.

Feature/persona Vs Persona (#17)

* feat: Persona vs Persona debate (Phase 2)

Backend:
- DebateAgent class: each twin is an independent agent with its own
  persona-consistent system prompt and conversation history. Agents
  respond via Bedrock converse, run in asyncio.to_thread to avoid
  blocking the event loop.
- POST /chat/debate: authenticated, validates both twins belong to the
  calling user, runs DEBATE_ROUNDS=3 rounds (6 total turns), returns
  full debate as JSON. Note: responses are buffered (API Gateway +
  Lambda proxy); true token streaming is a planned upgrade via Lambda
  Function URLs.
- Terraform: add POST /chat/debate API Gateway route.

Frontend:
- /debate page: twin selector (user-owned twins only, filtered to
  prevent selecting the same twin twice), topic input, start button.
- Progressive turn reveal: turns animate in one at a time (1.2s
  interval) with typing indicator between reveals.
- Two-avatar layout: Twin A (purple/blue) on left, Twin B (rose/orange)
  on right.
- Dashboard header: Debate link added next to UserButton.

commit.message:
chat: replace chatSessionCustomizations with chatSessionCustomizationProvider API (#303017)

* feat: add chat.registerCustomizationProvider extension API

Introduces a new proposed extension API (chatCustomizationProvider) that
enables extensions to register as customization providers for the AI
Customization UI. This replaces core-based harness filtering with
extension-driven discovery.

Key changes:
- New proposed API: vscode.proposed.chatCustomizationProvider.d.ts
  - ChatCustomizationProvider, ChatCustomizationItem, ChatCustomizationType
  - chat.registerCustomizationProvider(id, metadata, provider)
- ExtHost/MainThread RPC bridge for provider registration
- ICustomizationHarnessService extended with registerExternalHarness()
  for dynamic harness registration from extensions
- IHarnessDescriptor.itemProvider for extension-driven item discovery
- AICustomizationListWidget falls through to provider when active
  harness has an itemProvider
- Unit tests for dynamic harness registration and lifecycle

The static CLI/Claude harness descriptors remain as fallback until
extensions adopt the new API.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review issues

- Register chatCustomizationProvider in extensionsApiProposals.ts
- Fix duplicate 'descriptor' variable in fetchItemsForSection
- Add missing IExternalCustomizationItemProvider import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make management editor reactive to dynamic harness registration

- Track availableHarnesses in autorun (not just activeHarness)
- Add ensureHarnessDropdown() to lazily create/remove the dropdown
  when harnesses are dynamically registered/unregistered
- Store sidebarContent and harnessDropdownContainer refs for
  dynamic dropdown management

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: address API review feedback for ChatCustomizationProvider

- Replace ChatCustomizationType enum with TaskGroup-style class pattern
  (static instances with string-backed ids, extensible via constructor)
- Rename provideCustomizations → provideChatCustomizations to match
  VS Code provider naming conventions
- Add comprehensive JSDoc explaining customization lifecycle and
  caching semantics (cached until onDidChange fires)
- Simplify type converter to use class id directly
- Bump proposal version to 2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: quality improvements for ChatCustomizationProvider plumbing

- Skip storage-based grouping for provider-backed items in the
  customization list widget. External providers manage their own
  items, so Workspace/User/Extension categories don't apply —
  render a flat sorted list instead.
- Use AICustomizationManagementSection constants instead of
  hardcoded string literals in hiddenSections mapping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: make storage optional on IAICustomizationListItem

External provider items don't have a storage origin — the provider
manages discovery, so Workspace/User/Extension categories don't
apply. Make the storage field optional:

- Provider items omit storage entirely (no fake PromptsStorage.local)
- Context key overlay only sets storage key when present
- Management editor falls back gracefully for provider items
- Debug panel accepts optional storage
- Built-in path (promptsService) is unchanged — items always have storage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: include external provider data in customization debug report

When the active harness has an external provider, the debug report
now shows the provider's raw items grouped by type, with name, URI,
and description for each item, plus a count of items matching the
current section. The promptsService stages are skipped since they
don't apply to provider-backed harnesses.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: rename ChatCustomization → ChatSessionCustomization per API review

Renames all types, methods, events, DTOs, and the proposal file to use
the ChatSession prefix as requested in API review feedback.

- ChatCustomizationType → ChatSessionCustomizationType
- ChatCustomizationItem → ChatSessionCustomizationItem
- ChatCustomizationProvider → ChatSessionCustomizationProvider
- provideChatCustomizations → provideChatSessionCustomizations
- onDidChangeChatCustomizations → onDidChangeChatSessionCustomizations
- registerCustomizationProvider → registerChatSessionCustomizationProvider
- Proposal: chatCustomizationProvider → chatSessionCustomizationProvider

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve rebase conflicts and remove old ChatSessionCustomizations API

Remove the old group-based ChatSessionCustomizations API from the merged
PR #304532, which is superseded by our new ChatSessionCustomizationProvider
API. The old API used groups, storageLocation, and commands on the
chatSessionsService path; the new API uses a flat item model on the
customizationHarnessService path.

Removed:
- IChatSessionCustomizationItem/Group DTOs from extHost.protocol.ts
- registerChatSessionCustomizationsProvider from extHostChatSessions.ts,
  mainThreadChatSessions.ts, extHost.api.impl.ts, chatSessionsProvider.d.ts
- ChatSessionCustomizations converter namespace from extHostTypeConverters.ts
- mapProviderItemToListItem and old group command fields from list widget

Fixed:
- registerContributedHarness → registerExternalHarness in fixtures and
  mainThreadChatSessions.ts
- Missing AGENT_MD_FILENAME import in customizationHarnessService.ts
- Constructor arg mismatch in browser customizationHarnessService.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove version number from new chatSessionCustomizationProvider proposal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: rename id → chatSessionType in registerChatSessionCustomizationProvider

Aligns the parameter name with the chatSessions API convention.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add AICustomizationManagementCreateMenuId for extension create actions

Extensions can now contribute create/add button actions to the
customizations management editor via contributes.menus targeting
'AICustomizationManagementCreate'. Use the aiCustomizationManagementSection
context key to scope commands to specific sections (agents, skills, etc.).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: register chat/customizations/create as extension menu contribution point

Extensions contribute to 'chat/customizations/create' in package.json
contributes.menus, gated by chatSessionCustomizationProvider proposal.
Uses MenuId.for() to avoid cross-layer import.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: scope create menu to active harness, replace built-in actions

Add aiCustomizationManagementHarness context key set to the active
harness ID. Extensions scope create menu contributions using
'when: aiCustomizationManagementHarness == myHarness'.

When a harness has menu-contributed create actions, they fully replace
the built-in create buttons for that section.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review feedback

- Provider items without storage are now read-only (not editable/deletable)
- Wrap provideChatSessionCustomizations in try/catch to handle extension errors
- Use menuItem.run() instead of commandService.executeCommand for menu actions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: gate customization provider registration on kill-switch setting

Registration is now blocked when chat.customizations.providerApi.enabled
is false (default), preventing providers from affecting the UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------
